result.summary: Contentful exposes REST APIs for Delivery, Preview, Management, Images, SCIM, User Management, and Experience, plus GraphQL Content API and the `contentful` CLI; auth is via bearer tokens, per-space access tokens, OAuth-app tokens, or CLI login depending on the surface, with Images public.

cf_cma_pat.label: Contentful Personal Access Token / Management token
cf_cma_pat.setup: Go to [Contentful web app](https://app.contentful.com/), open `Settings > API keys > Content management tokens` or the [Personal Access Tokens](https://app.contentful.com/account/profile/cma_tokens) area, and create a token. If you prefer CLI onboarding, run `contentful login`; for headless use, create a personal token and pass it to `contentful login --management-token <token>` or use it as a `Bearer` token against Contentful APIs.

cf_cda_token.label: Contentful CDA / Preview access token
cf_cda_token.setup: In the [Contentful web app](https://app.contentful.com/), create an API key for the relevant space and copy the delivery or preview access token. Contentful notes these tokens are per-space and can be used either in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header or as `access_token=<token>` query parameter. Use delivery tokens for CDA and preview tokens for the Preview API.

cf_oauth_app.label: Contentful OAuth application credentials
cf_oauth_app.setup: If you need delegated access via OAuth, create an OAuth application in the Contentful web app under the OAuth applications area, then follow the app registration flow there. Contentful documents OAuth as an authentication option for the Content Management API; use this when you want user-consented access instead of a personal access token.

cf_cli_login.label: Contentful CLI login session
cf_cli_login.setup: Install `contentful-cli`, then run `contentful login`. If you cannot use a browser, first create a management token in the web app, then run `contentful login --management-token <token>`. The CLI stores the resulting login on your machine for subsequent commands.
